ideas.title: The Invisible System That Gets Lost Airline Bags Back to You
ideas.summary: A documentary-style look at the tracking, sorting, routing, and recovery networks that move delayed luggage across airports and airlines.
ideas.target_audience: Travelers, aviation enthusiasts, logistics fans, and viewers curious about airport operations.
ideas.potential: Air travel is globally relatable, lost luggage creates instant curiosity, and the topic reveals a hidden logistics system most passengers never see.

ideas.title: How Supermarkets Know What to Restock Before You Notice It Is Gone
ideas.summary: An exploration of the data systems, suppliers, warehouses, and shelf-level decisions that keep grocery stores continuously stocked.
ideas.target_audience: General viewers, retail workers, business students, supply chain enthusiasts, and everyday shoppers.
ideas.potential: Grocery shopping is universal, and the behind-the-scenes mix of data, logistics, and consumer behavior has strong evergreen appeal.

ideas.title: Inside the Secret Life of Shipping Containers After They Leave the Port
ideas.summary: A deep dive into how shipping containers are tracked, routed, reused, repaired, stored, and repositioned across global trade networks.
ideas.target_audience: Logistics enthusiasts, business audiences, global trade viewers, and documentary fans.
ideas.potential: Containers are iconic but poorly understood, and their hidden movement connects global commerce, ports, railways, trucking, and warehouses.

ideas.title: The Hidden Network That Keeps Traffic Lights in Sync
ideas.summary: A look at the sensors, control rooms, algorithms, and maintenance systems that coordinate traffic signals in modern cities.
ideas.target_audience: Urbanism fans, drivers, city planning audiences, technology viewers, and infrastructure enthusiasts.
ideas.potential: Traffic affects almost everyone, and revealing the systems behind signal timing turns a familiar frustration into a fascinating infrastructure story.

ideas.title: How Hospitals Move Patients, Medicine, and Equipment Without Chaos
ideas.summary: An investigation into the hidden logistics that coordinate beds, pharmacies, labs, sterile supplies, medical devices, and patient flow inside hospitals.
ideas.target_audience: Healthcare workers, operations enthusiasts, medical students, and viewers interested in complex systems.
ideas.potential: Hospitals are high-stakes environments where invisible coordination matters, creating strong emotional and educational value.

ideas.title: The Quiet Technology Behind Every Card Payment Approval
ideas.summary: A step-by-step documentary on the payment networks, fraud checks, authorization systems, and settlement processes behind a simple card tap.
ideas.target_audience: Consumers, fintech audiences, business viewers, tech enthusiasts, and finance students.
ideas.potential: Card payments are daily habits for millions, and the hidden chain behind a two-second approval is both surprising and highly relevant.

ideas.title: How Streaming Platforms Deliver a Movie to Millions Without Breaking
ideas.summary: An explanation of content delivery networks, caching, encoding, data centers, and traffic management that make large-scale streaming possible.
ideas.target_audience: Streaming users, tech viewers, software learners, media industry followers, and documentary audiences.
ideas.potential: Streaming is a massive everyday behavior, and the infrastructure behind instant playback offers strong visual and technical storytelling opportunities.

ideas.title: The Hidden Cold Chain That Keeps Vaccines, Food, and Medicine Safe
ideas.summary: A documentary on refrigerated logistics, temperature monitoring, specialized packaging, and distribution networks that protect sensitive goods.
ideas.target_audience: Science viewers, logistics fans, healthcare audiences, food industry professionals, and general documentary fans.
ideas.potential: Cold chains connect health, food, and global supply systems, making the topic both important and broadly understandable.

ideas.title: What Happens to Your Online Order in the First 60 Minutes
ideas.summary: A behind-the-scenes look at order routing, inventory allocation, warehouse picking, packing, labeling, and carrier handoff immediately after checkout.
ideas.target_audience: Online shoppers, ecommerce entrepreneurs, logistics viewers, warehouse workers, and business audiences.
ideas.potential: Ecommerce is universally familiar, and focusing on the first hour creates a clear, clickable frame for explaining a complex fulfillment system.

ideas.title: The Unseen Maintenance Systems That Stop Cities from Falling Apart
ideas.summary: An exploration of inspection schedules, repair crews, asset management software, emergency response teams, and preventive maintenance behind urban infrastructure.
ideas.target_audience: Urbanism audiences, infrastructure fans, city residents, engineering viewers, and public policy followers.
ideas.potential: The topic reframes everyday city life around the invisible work that prevents breakdowns, making it evergreen, scalable, and highly documentary-friendly.
